description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, angular profile and a predominantly gray coloration. The surface is uneven, featuring a mix of flat and jagged planes, with some areas appearing slightly smoother due to natural weathering. There are visible chips and minor fractures along the edges, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements for some time. A small, white label is affixed to one face, indicating it may have been cataloged or studied. The stone's mass and density are typical of sedimentary or metamorphic rock, and it appears solid throughout. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, gray stone with an irregular, angular shape and rough, weathered surfaces. It features minor chips, a catalog label, and is solid and dense.
description_view_0: This view shows the stone's broadest, flattest face with visible surface texture and a prominent angular ridge.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the stone's narrow, pointed end and the attached white label on the upper surface.
description_view_2: The side profile reveals the stone's length and a ruler for scale, emphasizing its rough, blocky structure.
description_view_3: This view displays the stone's tapered end and the uneven, sloped top surface.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or natural barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle natural mottling and minor veining, with no artificial carvings or regular patterns
attribute_weathering: Rough with minor chips and surface wear, indicating moderate exposure to weather
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements
